title: archangelica officinalis
intro: Archangelica officinalis, also known as angelica, is a biennial or perennial plant of the parsley family, native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its tall stems, large leaves, and umbrella-like clusters of small white flowers. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, due to its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural diuretic. The health benefits of angelica include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate menstrual cramps, and support the immune system. Angelica can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as tea, tincture, or dried root, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
